Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Correcting Clerical Error On Face Of Order After Hearing Entered December 22, 2021 On Plaintif'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-09-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
KENNETH PRITIKIN, ESQ., LAW OFFICE OF KENNETH PRITIKIN, (510) 710-7136, kwpritikin@gmail.com, for Plaintiff ESFIR VINER,
SARAH N. LEGER, ESQ., RAGGHIANTI FREITAS LLP, (415) 453-9433, for Other Party Kim Schwarcz, Personal Representative of the Estate of David J. McMahon,

PLAINTIFF ESFIR VINER'S Motion For Order Correcting Clerical Error On Face Of Order After Hearing Entered December 22, 2021 On Plaintif'S Motion For Summary Adjudication.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Esfir Viner's ("Esfir") motion for order correcting clerical error is denied. First, Esfir seeks to remedy a purported judicial error rather than a clerical error. "The distinction between a clerical error and a judicial error does not depend so much on the person making it as it does on whether it was the deliberate result of judicial reasoning and determination." (Estate of Doane (1964) 62 Cal.2d 68, 71.) The court has no power "to set aside or amend judicial errors except under appropriate statutory proceedings." (Id.) Second, this motion is an untimely and substantively defective motion for reconsideration in violation of CCP 1008. Third, the summary adjudications of Esfir's issues 13 and 17 runs afoul of the amendments of the summary adjudication statute. (See Paramount Petroleum Corp. v. Superior Court (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 226, 241-242.) Esfir is attempting to summarily adjudicate factual issues under the guise of duty and/or not completely disposing of the issue of duty by attempting to parse it up. (See CCP 437c(f)(1).) In the prior Boyko motion (case #569759), the plaintiff sought summary adjudication on the issue of duty as to all of the work she performed. In this case, the court cannot summarily adjudicate an issue of duty in a piecemeal fashion under CCP 437c(f)(1).

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

SARAH N. LEGER, ESQ., RAGGHIANTI FREITAS LLP, (415) 453-9433, for Other Party Kim Schwarcz, Personal Representative of the Estate of David J. McMahon,

PLAINTIFF ESFIR VINER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Esfir Viner's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4. The attempt to summarily adjudicate these issues runs afoul of the amendments of the summary adjudication statute. (See Paramount Petroleum Corp. v. Superior Court (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 226, 241-242.) Plaintiff is attempting to summarily adjudicate factual issues under the guise of duty and/or not completely disposing of the issue of duty by attempting to parse it up. (See CCP 437c(f)(1).) The court cannot summarily adjudicate an issue of duty in a piecemeal fashion under CCP 437c(f)(1).

Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 5-9. Plaintiff failed to maintain her initial burden of production per CCP 437(p)(1). Plaintiff does not clearly set forth the total amount of her claimed damages.

The court notes that defendants refer to the Leger declaration, but it was never filed with the court.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
